title: Main Idea
description:
Passage:

'The economic theories of John Maynard Keynes, developed during the Great Depression, argued that active government intervention in the marketplace was necessary to stabilize the economy. Keynes suggested that during recessions, aggregate demand drops, leading to high unemployment. To counteract this, governments should increase public spending and lower taxes to stimulate demand and pull the economy out of stagnation.'

According to the passage, what was the primary purpose of Keynes's theories?
options: To discourage government involvement in business
To explain how to stabilize the economy through government intervention
To argue for higher taxes during periods of inflation
To promote a purely free-market capitalist system
correctAnswer: To explain how to stabilize the economy through government intervention
explanation: The text states that Keynes 'argued that active government intervention in the marketplace was necessary to stabilize the economy.' This matches the option.

title: Detail Recall
description:
Passage:

'The economic theories of John Maynard Keynes, developed during the Great Depression, argued that active government intervention in the marketplace was necessary to stabilize the economy. Keynes suggested that during recessions, aggregate demand drops, leading to high unemployment. To counteract this, governments should increase public spending and lower taxes to stimulate demand and pull the economy out of stagnation.'

According to Keynes, what is the direct consequence of a drop in aggregate demand during a recession?
options: Lower public spending
Higher taxes
High unemployment
Increased market stabilization
explanation: The passage explicitly says: 'during recessions, aggregate demand drops, leading to high unemployment.'

title: Inference Question
description:
Passage:

'The economic theories of John Maynard Keynes, developed during the Great Depression, argued that active government intervention in the marketplace was necessary to stabilize the economy. Keynes suggested that during recessions, aggregate demand drops, leading to high unemployment. To counteract this, governments should increase public spending and lower taxes to stimulate demand and pull the economy out of stagnation.'

What can be inferred about Keynes's view on government budgets during a recession?
options: Governments should aim to achieve a budget surplus
Governments must balance their budgets at all costs
Governments should tolerate deficits to boost the economy
Governments should stop spending altogether
correctAnswer: Governments should tolerate deficits to boost the economy
explanation: Keynes suggests that governments should 'increase public spending and lower taxes' during a recession. Increasing spending while reducing tax revenue naturally leads to a budget deficit. Therefore, we can infer he believes governments should run deficits during recessions to stimulate the economy.

title: Contextual Assumption
description:
Passage:

'The economic theories of John Maynard Keynes, developed during the Great Depression, argued that active government intervention in the marketplace was necessary to stabilize the economy. Keynes suggested that during recessions, aggregate demand drops, leading to high unemployment. To counteract this, governments should increase public spending and lower taxes to stimulate demand and pull the economy out of stagnation.'

What underlying assumption does Keynes make about the relationship between government action and consumer behavior?
options: Consumers will save all additional money received from tax cuts
Government spending has no impact on total market demand
Stimulating demand through government action is effective in altering economic outcomes
Market forces are self-correcting and do not respond to external stimuli
correctAnswer: Stimulating demand through government action is effective in altering economic outcomes
explanation: Keynes assumes that increasing public spending and lowering taxes will successfully 'stimulate demand and pull the economy out of stagnation'. This assumes that government action is capable of changing the trajectory of consumer demand and economic health.

title: Critical Evaluation
description:
Passage:

'The economic theories of John Maynard Keynes, developed during the Great Depression, argued that active government intervention in the marketplace was necessary to stabilize the economy. Keynes suggested that during recessions, aggregate demand drops, leading to high unemployment. To counteract this, governments should increase public spending and lower taxes to stimulate demand and pull the economy out of stagnation.'

Which of the following scenarios, if true, would most seriously weaken the effectiveness of the Keynesian approach during a recession?
options: Increased government spending leads to immediate private sector hiring
Lowering taxes causes consumers to hoard cash rather than spend it
Interest rates remain low as a result of stable government policies
Public infrastructure projects create long term productive assets for the country
correctAnswer: Lowering taxes causes consumers to hoard cash rather than spend it
explanation: The core of the Keynesian argument is that lowering taxes will 'stimulate demand' (i.e. consumers will spend more, buying goods and services). If consumers hoard cash instead of spending it, demand will not increase, and the policy will fail to pull the economy out of stagnation.
